---
title: "Installation"
---

## Prerequisites {#sec-installation}

ferx has two layers of dependencies:

| Layer | Required | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| R ≥ 4.2 | Yes | Package interface |
| Rust / Cargo | Yes | Compile the ferx-core backend |
| Enzyme (LLVM) | No | Exact automatic differentiation for faster gradients |

### Installing Rust

If you do not have Rust, install it via [rustup](https://rustup.rs):

```bash
curl --proto '=https' --tlsv1.2 -sSf https://sh.rustup.rs | sh
```

Verify:

```bash
rustc --version   #> rustc 1.78.0 (9b00956e5 2024-04-29) or newer
cargo --version   #> cargo 1.78.0 ...
```

ferx requires the **nightly** toolchain for Enzyme builds, but a stable toolchain is sufficient when autodiff is disabled (see @sec-no-autodiff).

## Installing ferx-r

With Rust in your `PATH`, install from GitHub:

```r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pkg_install("FeRx-NLME/ferx-r")
```

Or with `remotes`:

```r
remotes::install_github("FeRx-NLME/ferx-r")
```

The install compiles the Rust backend, which takes 1–3 minutes on a modern machine.
Subsequent installs reuse Cargo's artifact cache and are faster.

### Verify the installation

```r
library(ferx)
ferx_check_init()
```

A successful output looks like:

```
✓ ferx backend loaded
✓ Rust backend version: 0.x.y
```

## The FERX_NO_AUTODIFF flag {#sec-no-autodiff}

By default, ferx uses [Enzyme](https://enzyme.mit.edu) to compute exact gradients via automatic differentiation of the Rust source.
This requires a nightly Rust toolchain and LLVM support.

If you just want to evaluate ferx without the full Enzyme setup, set:

```bash
FERX_NO_AUTODIFF=1
```

before installation:

```r
Sys.setenv(FERX_NO_AUTODIFF = "1")
remotes::install_github("FeRx-NLME/ferx-r")
```

In this mode ferx falls back to **finite-difference gradients**, which are slightly slower but otherwise produce identical estimates.
All examples in this book run with `FERX_NO_AUTODIFF=1` on CI.

## Setting up Enzyme (optional)

Enzyme unlocks exact gradients.
It requires:

1. A nightly Rust toolchain:

```bash
rustup toolchain install nightly
rustup override set nightly
```

2. LLVM ≥ 17 on your system PATH.

3. Unset `FERX_NO_AUTODIFF` (or remove it from your environment), then reinstall:

```r
Sys.unsetenv("FERX_NO_AUTODIFF")
remotes::install_github("FeRx-NLME/ferx-r")
```

If Enzyme is available the install log will contain:

```
[build] autodiff: Enzyme backend enabled
```

## Docker / reproducible environments

A minimal `Dockerfile` is included in the ferx-r repository.
It installs Rust + the package in a stable, reproducible layer suitable for CI:

```dockerfile
FROM rocker/r-ver:4.4.0
ENV FERX_NO_AUTODIFF=1
RUN Rscript -e "remotes::install_github('FeRx-NLME/ferx-r')"
```

For production workflows where runtime matters, use the Enzyme-enabled image documented in the repo README.
